body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Granny autonomous dispatch loop
body.intention: I intend to verify that the autonomous dispatch loop does not silently regress ticket integrity, double-dispatch, or enable runaway token spend.
body.description:
**What changed:** Granny now dispatches CC and DickSimnel autonomously end-to-end via bus handshake, with stale-ticket watchdogs and dispatched-set pruning removing previous blocks.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- Prune-then-dispatch loop: if _load_dispatched() prunes a ticket that was dispatched 5s ago (before DB status updates from dispatched→in_progress), Granny could re-dispatch it on the next cycle. The _cc0_busy() DB check should prevent this, but the window is narrow.
- Stale-in_progress watchdog fires during active sprint: if CC is mid-sprint and takes >2h on a single ticket, the watchdog resets it to sprint mid-execution. CC finishes and closes it — no harm, but the setstatus sprint creates a redundant dispatch window.
- IDLE listener tmux injection during interactive session: deliver_fn fires send-keys into claude-main while Akien is in a design conversation. Same risk as old tmux path but now harder to trace because dispatch source is the IMAP listener, not Granny directly.
- Repeated escalation loop: broken ticket (always fails mid-sprint) → escalated → manual reset to sprint → dispatched again → loop. No hard max-retry counter exists.

**Signals to watch:**
- Channel: GRANNY_DISPATCH appearing twice for the same ticket within one poll cycle
- Granny log: 'dispatched T-xxx' appearing twice without an intervening 'closed T-xxx'
- Token spend: >200k/day increase in OR cost without corresponding closed tickets
- Ticket: any ticket cycling between in_progress → sprint → in_progress more than 3 times

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-18 or after 10 autonomous dispatches complete.

**Worst-case token spend:**
  - Per-failure cost: ~50k-200k tokens per full sprint attempt on a broken ticket
  - Hard cap: cc_queue.py reset --timeout trips gate at 3 resets → ticket auto-held (T-granny-stale-inprogress-watchdog uses this)
  - Blast radius: capped at 3 automatic retries × ~150k tokens ≈ 450k tokens max before ticket is held; circuit breaker is the existing --timeout counter in cc_queue.py

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** no automated verification — observation ticket; done when predicted effects are annotated as occurred / clear / partial and ticket is closed
